title: chore(sdk)!: remove speculative batch/progress/browser helpers from extras
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Remove speculative, never-used public exports from the SDK `extras` module:

- Drop `generateImages`, `generateVideos`, `generateBatch`/`BatchResult`, `generateImageWithProgress`/`AwaitOptions`/`ProgressStatus`, `showImage`, `displayImage` (verified zero internal callers via full-repo grep)
- Keep `wrapImageResponse`/`wrapVideoResponse`/`wrapAudioResponse`/`wrapChatResponse`, `Conversation`, and `arrayBufferToBase64` (still used by the kept wrappers)
- The batch / multiple-image use case remains available via the documented `generateImage({ n })` / `imageGenerate({ n })`

**Breaking (public API):** version bumped 4.4.0 → 4.5.0 with a CHANGELOG "Removed (Breaking)" entry. Net −237 lines; tsup build (incl. DTS) succeeds, 24/24 tests pass.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Pruning the SDK
gist.blurb: We've pruned some unused, speculative helper functions from the SDK's extras module to keep the codebase light and focused.
gist.summary: Removed unused speculative helpers like generateImages, generateBatch, and generateImageWithProgress from the SDK's extras module, bumping the version to 4.5.0.
gist.impact: Developers get a leaner SDK with a cleaner API surface, while batch generation remains fully supported via the standard generateImage({ n }) parameter.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a gardener, pruning a digital code-plant to keep it healthy. Unused code blocks falling away into a compost bin.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny gardener's apron, pruning a digital code-plant in a warm garden shed. Small pixelated code blocks fall gently into a wooden compost bin. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley.
